metadata.summary: Figma AI plugins automate repetitive design tasks, but agents cannot trigger them. The Figma REST API is the actual agent integration surface for automated pipelines.

content.title: AI Plugin Ecosystem
content.summary: Figma AI plugins (Magician, Automator, Content Reel, Stark, Similayer, Diagram) automate repetitive design tasks within the Figma canvas. Critically, agents cannot trigger plugin runs programmatically — the Figma REST API is the actual agent integration surface. Use plugins for human-driven workflows; use the REST API for automated pipelines.

why.p: Most Figma AI plugins have no CLI or webhook interface; they operate only inside the Figma desktop/browser UI. An agent that "runs a plugin" always requires a human at the keyboard. The REST API offers a deterministic, repeatable integration surface for accessibility audits, content injection, and style extraction — the tasks designers most want to automate.

list.item: Automating repetitive Figma operations (renaming layers, bulk style changes, exporting assets) via Figma REST API
Running accessibility audits across a design file before handoff (contrast ratio computation from REST API data)
Generating placeholder content at scale and pushing it via Figma REST `PATCH /v1/files/{key}/nodes`
Evaluating which plugin combination covers a team's workflow gaps before investing in custom UXP development
Running WCAG contrast checks externally without requiring the Stark plugin
Plugin output must be reproducible without human intervention — most AI plugins are not deterministic
Design decisions require brand or strategic judgment — plugins are automation tools, not decision-makers
A plugin relies on a third-party AI API that could expose proprietary design data to external servers
The design system is unstable — plugins that reference tokens/styles break when foundations change
Magician — AI-powered icon/image generation (UI-only, no API)
Automator — batch operations with AI (UI-only, no external API)
Content Reel — AI text and image placeholders (UI-only; use REST write API as alternative)
Similayer — smart layer selection helper (no external API surface)
Diagram — AI diagramming (UI-only)
Stark — accessibility checking (standalone CLI for HTML audits; Figma integration is UI-only)

rule.statement: Treat Figma plugins as human-operated tools and the Figma REST API as the agent integration boundary.
rule.rationale: No Figma plugin exposes a programmatic invocation endpoint. Any workflow described as "agent runs plugin" requires a human at the keyboard. The REST API is repeatable and deterministic.

rule.statement: Maintain a curated plugin list per project in AGENTS.md to prevent unauthorized plugin installations.
rule.rationale: Plugin sandbox does not prevent plugins from calling external APIs. Unreviewed plugins may send proprietary design data to third-party servers. Review plugin source or privacy policy before use with proprietary designs.

rule.statement: For batch operations that agents could handle, prefer the Figma REST API over plugin-based automation.
rule.rationale: REST API calls are version-controlled, auditable, and reproducible. Plugin outputs depend on AI model state and change across plugin updates without notice.

antipattern.description: Assuming REST write access can create new frames.
antipattern.reason: Figma REST API write access is restricted: agents cannot create new frames, only update existing node properties. Design-from-scratch automation requires a human setup step.

antipattern.description: Fetching the full file JSON for large files.
antipattern.reason: File JSON exports can be 10–200 MB for complex files. Always scope requests to specific node IDs with the `?ids=node_id` parameter to avoid memory issues and rate limit exhaustion.

rule.statement: Run both a plugin (Stark, during design) and an automated axe-core scan (post-handoff on rendered HTML) — neither alone is sufficient.
rule.rationale: Figma plugin audits catch design-phase issues; axe-core catches implementation-phase regressions. Accessibility plugins flag issues but cannot auto-fix them; a human must apply remediations.

rule.statement: Extract color fills from Figma REST API and compute WCAG contrast ratios in code, without requiring the Stark plugin.
rule.rationale: Agents cannot trigger plugin runs. Computing contrast ratios from the REST API node data achieves the same result deterministically and without a human Figma session.

example.note: Fetch file styles via REST, extract text/background fill pairs, compute relative luminance, flag pairs below WCAG thresholds.

antipattern.description: Using a read-only Figma token for write operations.
antipattern.reason: A read-only token silently fails write operations. Token scopes (read vs. write) must be set correctly per operation type.

antipattern.description: Logging plugin version alongside design outputs.
antipattern.reason: AI plugin outputs change with model updates. Without version logging, it is impossible to reproduce or audit which model state produced a given design artifact.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Agents cannot directly invoke Figma plugins — the plugin ecosystem operates inside the Figma canvas and requires a human-initiated session. The practical agentic pattern is to use the Figma REST API to read/write file data (nodes, styles, components), then post-process results externally with Claude. For accessibility, an agent can extract color contrast pairs via the API and run WCAG checks in code without needing the Stark plugin. For content generation (placeholders, copy), an agent produces content externally and pushes it via the Figma API's `PATCH /v1/files/{key}/nodes` endpoint. Reserve actual plugin use for human-driven workflows and use the REST API as the agent integration surface.

list.item: faion-sdd-executor-agent — executes Figma API calls as part of a design-system audit task
Custom accessibility-check agent — reads Figma fills/strokes via REST, computes WCAG contrast ratios, returns a violation report

section.name: Prompt Patterns
section.code:
You have access to a Figma file JSON export. Audit all text layers for: 1. Contrast ratio < 4.5:1 (normal text) or < 3:1 (large text) 2. Missing semantic role (decorative vs. informational not flagged)
Return a JSON array: [{node_id, name, issue, current_ratio, required_ratio}]

list.item: figma-export (community) — Export Figma frames/components to SVG/PNG via CLI; github.com/lucaorio/figma-export
figma-api (Node SDK) — Programmatic read/write to Figma REST API; npm i @figma/rest-api-spec
axe-core — WCAG accessibility audit on rendered HTML (post-handoff); npm i axe-core
color-contrast-checker (npm) — Compute WCAG contrast ratios offline; npm i color-contrast-checker
svg-to-ts — Convert exported SVG icons to typed TS components; npm i svg-to-ts
Figma REST API — SaaS, agent-friendly (REST); full read; limited write (nodes, styles, components). No plugin execution.
Magician — SaaS plugin, not agent-friendly; UI-only; no API or webhook. Agent cannot trigger.
Automator (Figma plugin) — SaaS plugin, not agent-friendly; UI-only batch ops; cannot be scripted externally.
Stark (accessibility) — SaaS plugin + standalone, partial agent-friendly; standalone Stark CLI available for HTML audits; Figma integration is UI-only.
Content Reel — SaaS plugin, not agent-friendly; content injection UI-only; use Figma REST write API as alternative.
Similayer — OSS plugin, not agent-friendly; layer-selection helper; no external API surface.

section.name: Figma Accessibility Contrast Checker Example

list.item: Treat Figma plugins as human-operated tools and the Figma REST API as the agent integration boundary
Maintain a curated plugin list per project in AGENTS.md to prevent team members from installing unreviewed plugins that could leak design IP
For accessibility, run both a plugin (Stark, during design) and an automated axe-core scan (post-handoff, on rendered HTML) — neither alone is sufficient
When evaluating a new AI plugin, test on a disposable copy of the file, not the main design system
Log which plugin version produced which design output for auditability — AI plugin outputs change with model updates
For batch operations that agents could handle, prefer the Figma REST API over plugin-based automation to maintain repeatability
There is no way for an agent to trigger a Figma plugin run; any workflow described as "agent runs plugin" requires a human at the keyboard
Figma REST API write access is restricted: you cannot create new frames, only update existing node properties
File JSON exports from Figma REST can be large (10–200 MB for complex files); agents must paginate or scope requests to specific node IDs
Plugin sandbox isolates execution — plugins cannot call external APIs unless the plugin author has set up a proxy; proprietary prompts in plugin fields may be sent to third-party servers
Figma token scopes (read vs. write) must be set correctly; a read-only token will silently fail write operations
The Figma Community plugin listing does not indicate whether a plugin sends data externally; review plugin source code or privacy policy before use with proprietary designs
When plugin output must be reproducible without human intervention — most AI plugins are not deterministic
When the team lacks a Figma Organization or Professional plan (some plugins require paid tiers)
When design decisions require brand or strategic judgment — plugins are automation tools, not decision-makers
When a plugin relies on a third-party AI API that could expose proprietary design data to external servers
When the design system is unstable — plugins that reference tokens/styles break when foundations change
Figma REST API does not expose a plugin execution endpoint; agents cannot trigger plugin runs programmatically from outside Figma
AI content generated by plugins (Magician, etc.) is non-deterministic — same prompt yields different results across runs
Most Figma AI plugins have no CLI or webhook interface; they only operate within the Figma desktop/browser UI
Plugin data access is sandboxed — they can read/write the current file but cannot batch-process multiple files
Accessibility plugins (Stark) flag issues but cannot auto-fix them; a human must apply remediations
Plugin API rate limits and quota resets are opaque; heavily automated usage risks account suspension

template.purpose: WCAG contrast ratio computation from Figma REST API data
Claude prompt for auditing Figma file JSON for contrast violations
